title: Inkling: Our Open-Weights Model
author: vimarsh6739
relevanceReason: 命中 3 个 AI/科技关键词。
summaryRaw: Our mission is to build AI that extends human will and judgment. We have developed a platform that lets anyone customize models, previewed an AI system built for interactive collaboration, and published novel research. Today we are advancing our mission by releasing a model we trained from scratch with the full weights available, so that people can make it their own. Our model, called Inkling, is a Mixture-of-Experts transformer with 975B total parameters, 41B active. It supports a context window of up to 1M tokens. It was pretrained on 45 trillion tokens of text, images, audio and video. It is the first in a family of models of different sizes: alongside it we are sharing a preview of Inkling-Small, a lighter-weight model with 12B active parameters, trained with a similar recipe, that achieves strong performance with even lower cost and latency. Inkling reasons natively over text, images, and audio, and balances cost with performance through efficient and controllable thinking effort. We trained it to be a broad, balanced foundation model: strong across many domains, flexible enough to adapt. Inkling is not the strongest overall model available today, open or closed. Instead, a combination of qualities makes it a good open-weights base for customization: multimodal capabilities, efficient thinking, and availability on Tinker for fine-tuning. Inkling is just the start: our first release in a model family we will continue to build on. We want to make customization accessible for more use cases, so Inkling is available for fine-tuning on Tinker today. Picking the right base model to fine-tune is a qualitative judgment that combines measurable benchmarks with the unique feel of a model that comes from playing with it. To enable the latter we’re adding the Inkling Playground in the Tinker console: a developer-facing interface for chatting with Inkling. To show what customization means in practice, we asked Inkling to fine-tune itself. Using Tinker, the model wrote its own fine-tuning job, ran it, and evaluated the result:
start in OpenCode: Inkling runs inside the OpenCode harness.

Capabilities Real-world applications require models with a wide range of capabilities that can be combined and improved with fine-tuning. We showcase what Inkling can do and how it measures up on important qualities such as trustworthiness and safety. Generalist model Inkling is designed to be broad. We trained it across agentic, reasoning, coding, instruction-following, factuality, vision, and audio tasks, rather than narrowly optimizing for one domain. That breadth matters for customization and real-world use: different users need models that can adapt to very different workflows, not just excel on benchmarks.

Spider chart comparing Inkling, Nemotron 3 Ultra, GLM 5.2, GPT 5.6 Sol, and Claude Fable 5 on ten evaluations scored from zero to one hundred. Inkling is shown with the heavier cobalt line. Evaluations without a reported model score are plotted at zero. Hover an evaluation to compare every model's score.

comments.author: segmondy
comments.textRawHtml:
Very nice, multi modal, largest open weight model that supports audio. Would be interesting to see how good the audio capability is.

If you want to run locally, checkout https://github.com/danielhanchen/llama.cpp/tree/add-inkling https://unsloth.ai/docs/models/inkling https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-GGUF https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-NVFP4

This supposedly is better than KimiK2.7, as much hype as GLM5.2 gets, I find myself using KimiK2.7 half of the time, so if the benchmark is true, then this can definitely go in the mix. My hope is that it might have strengths in some areas to beat all other open weight models.
comments.textZhHtml:
非常好，多模态，支持音频的最大开放权重模型。 看看音频功能有多好会很有趣。

如果您想在本地运行，请查看 https://github.com/danielhanchen/llama.cpp/tree/add-inkling https://unsloth.ai/docs/models/inkling https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-GGUF https://huggingface.co/unsloth/inkling-NVFP4

这据说比 KimiK2.7 更好，与 GLM5.2 一样大肆宣传，我发现自己有一半的时间使用 KimiK2.7，所以如果基准测试是正确的，那么这绝对可以加入其中。 我希望它在某些领域具有击败所有其他开放重量模型的优势。

children.author: paxys
children.textRawHtml: Not to mention - it is American. This is the first competitive non-Chinese open weights model since what, Llama 3?
children.textZhHtml: 更不用说 - 这是美国的。这是自 Llama 3 以来第一个非中国公开重量级竞争模型

children.author: Topfi
children.textRawHtml:
North Mini Code by Cohere (HQd in Toronto) has honestly been very competitive in my personal assessment with many of the models coming out of the PRC. I'd position it below Moonshot AIs and Z.ais recent releases, but above the varieties of Qwen, Deepseek, MiMo, etc.

Depends whether America the continent or just the United States counts of course.
children.textZhHtml:
Cohere（总部位于多伦多）的 North Mini Code 在我的个人评估中确实非常有竞争力，许多来自中国的型号。我将其定位在 Moonshot AI 和 Z.ais 最近发布的版本之下，但高于 Qwen、Deepseek、MiMo 等版本。

当然取决于美国大陆还是仅美国。

children.author: AlanYx
children.textRawHtml:
>Cohere (HQd in Toronto)

Cohere keeps changing their story about where they're headquartered. From 2019-2020 they were HQed in Toronto. Then from early 2020 to 2026 they billed themselves as dual-headquartered in Toronto and San Francisco. Then in April 2026 they started to bill themselves as dual headquartered in Toronto and Berlin. Sometime in the last two months they've started to bill themselves as HQed in Toronto again.
children.textZhHtml:
>Cohere（总部位于多伦多）

Cohere 一直在改变其关于总部所在地的说法。2019 年至 2020 年，他们的总部设在多伦多。然后从 2020 年初到 2026 年，他们宣称在多伦多和旧金山设有双总部。接着在 2026 年 4 月，他们又开始宣称在多伦多和柏林设有双总部。而在过去两个月里的某个时候，他们又改口称总部设在多伦多了。

children.author: andy99
children.textRawHtml:
Interesting, I didn’t know this existed, do you think it’s competitive with Qwen 3.6 35B A3B which seems to be the closest comparator?

It’s 20 vs 32 in favor of Qwen on artificial analysis intelligence index (cohere isn’t benchmarked on the coding index)
children.textZhHtml:
有意思，我之前不知道还有这个模型，你觉得它能和 Qwen 3.6 35B A3B 竞争吗？后者似乎是最接近的对标对象。

在 artificial analysis 智能指数上，Qwen 以 32 比 20 领先（Cohere 没有参与编程指数的基准测试）

children.author: throwa356262
children.textRawHtml:
It is surprisingly capable for coding despite its tiny size.

You can try it out for free:

https://opencode.ai/docs/zen/#pricing
children.textZhHtml:
尽管体积小巧，但它的编程能力却出人意料地强大。

您可以免费试用：

https://opencode.ai/docs/zen/#pricing

children.author: segmondy
children.textRawHtml:
It's not the better model since Llama3. Trinity Large is American and quite decent, unfortunately tons of crazy good models have been out and it's harder to run locally at 400B. I think Arcee did a terrible job of promoting their model.

https://www.arcee.ai/blog/trinity-large
children.textZhHtml:
这不是自 Llama3 以来更好的模型。 Trinity Large是美国的，相当不错，不幸的是，大量疯狂的好模型已经问世，在本地运行400B比较困难。 我认为 Arcee 在推广他们的模型方面做得很糟糕。

https://www.arcee.ai/blog/trinity-large

children.author: mark_l_watson
children.textRawHtml:
+1 I enthusiastically use Chinese open weight models for a wide range of tasks (I also love Opus and Gemini) but I am so happy to see another high quality American open model (I consider gemma to be high quality, like qwen).

I enjoyed turning off web search for Inkling to experiment with what innate knowledge is encoded in the model weights. A fun thing I do is check what innate knowledge very large models contain about me, as an individual. Inkling has an interesting concise shadow of what I do. (I have written a lot of books, so I am in training data.)
children.textZhHtml:
+1 我热衷于将中国开放权重模型用于各种任务（我也喜欢 Opus 和 Gemini），但我很高兴看到另一个高质量的美国开放模型（我认为 gemma 质量很高，比如 qwen）。

我喜欢关闭 Inkling 的网络搜索来试验模型权重中编码的先天知识。我做的一件有趣的事情是检查非常大的模型包含关于我作为个人的先天知识。 Inkling 对我所做的事情有一个有趣而简洁的影子。 （我写了很多书，所以我在训练数据。）

children.author: throwaw12
children.textRawHtml:
What does it mean, if it is American?

Is it censored or will it eventually stop working in Middle Eastern countries?

Or is it biased towards powerful political lobby group interests?

When weights are open I usually don't care where is it from, as long as it is working for my use cases well
children.textZhHtml:
如果它是美国的，这意味着什么？

它是否受到审查，或者最终会在中东国家停止工作？

或者它是否偏向强大的政治游说团体利益？

当权重开放时，我通常不在乎它来自哪里，只要它适合我的用例即可

children.author: swiftcoder
children.textRawHtml: There's a lot of Sinophobia in the tech world, and the AI race seems to be magnifying that. With very little evidence to back it up, I might add - we've had regular releases of quality open-weight models from Chinese firms, and no sign they are more censored/ideological, than, say, Grok
children.textZhHtml: 科技界存在很多恐华情绪，而 AI 竞赛似乎正在放大这种情绪。而且我要补充一点，这几乎没有什么证据支持——中国企业一直在定期发布高质量的开源权重模型，也没有迹象表明它们比 Grok 等模型受到更多审查或更具意识形态色彩。

children.author: Saline9515
children.textRawHtml: The Chinese are quite open about their efforts regarding intellectual espionage and the desire for control of other countries' supply chains. It's not sinophobia to be cautious about it. It doesn't mean that the US companies are angels either.
children.textZhHtml: 中方对其在知识产权间谍活动方面的努力以及控制他国供应链的企图毫不掩饰。对此保持警惕并非仇华心理作祟。但这也不意味着美国企业就都是清白无辜的。

children.author: mastermage
children.textRawHtml: Only actualy censored thing i found so far was Tianmen Square. And depending on what you use it for thats realy not a big issue.
children.textZhHtml: 到目前为止，我发现唯一真正被审查的内容是天安门广场。而且这取决于你的用途，这其实不是什么大问题。

children.author: Saline9515
children.textRawHtml: Any discussion about the Chinese Communist Party is censored on Deepseek.
children.textZhHtml: 任何关于中国共产党的讨论在Deepseek上都会被审查。

children.author: smiths1999
children.textRawHtml: If the company does any kind of work with the US Government it's far easier to just say no to Chinese-origin models rather than deal with all the overhead on the government contracts. Since just about every major tech company has US contracts they all have to be careful about the use of Chinese models.
children.textZhHtml: 如果公司与美国政府有任何形式的合作，直接拒绝使用中国背景的模型要比处理政府合同中所有的繁文缛节容易得多。由于几乎所有大型科技公司都有美国政府的合同，因此它们在使用中国模型时都必须非常谨慎。

children.author: adrian_b
children.textRawHtml:
There is also NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra, with 561B parameters, which was released a month ago.

I do not know yet how smart it is, but the NVIDIA LLMs are very well optimized for fast inference (on their GPUs of course).

Previously that was the biggest American open-weights LLM.
children.textZhHtml:
还有一个月前发布的 NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra，参数为 561B。

我还不知道它有多智能，但是 NVIDIA LLM 为快速推理进行了很好的优化（当然是在他们的 GPU 上）。

之前那是美国最大的开放权重 LLM。

children.author: berkes
children.textRawHtml: Mistral has released some very good, very useful open models. It's European, that's also non-Chinese.
children.textZhHtml: Mistral 发布了一些非常好的、非常有用的开放模型。这是欧洲的，也是非中国的。

children.author: kzrdude
children.textRawHtml: Google has released some very good and useful models too, Gemma, but they are not so large. But they are in useful size.
children.textZhHtml: 谷歌也发布了一些非常好用的模型，比如 Gemma，但它们的规模不算太大。不过尺寸很实用。
